headline: Carnival Reshapes Debt Listings While Elevating Cunard Luxury Experience
summary: Carnival Corporation & plc (NYSE:CCL) plans to voluntarily delist certain senior notes and debentures from the NYSE, the UK FCA official list, and the London Stock Exchange as part of its proposed unification of the dual-listed structure. The company links these capital markets moves to its broader corporate reorganization plans, aiming to simplify how its debt securities trade and are regulated. Separately, Cunard, Carnival's luxury brand, is partnering with celebrity stylist Micaela...

headline: Princess Cruises Announces 2027-28 South America Season Aboard Majestic Princess: Explore 19 Destinations, 15 UNESCO Sites, and Antarctica
summary: Princess Cruises unveils its 2027–28 South America season aboard Majestic Princess, featuring overnight experiences in the Antarctic Peninsula, late-night port calls, and access to 15 UNESCO World Heritage Sites across the region. On sale now, the October 2027 through January 2028 season includes six departures across four itineraries (15 – 37 days), designed to give guests more time ashore in marquee cities and closer-to-nature scenic cruising in Patagonia and beyond.

headline: Carnival Stock Cut by Barclays and Truist, but This Wall Street Pro Still Sees Upside
summary: Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) is heading into its March 27 Q1 2026 earnings report with two Wall Street firms moving in the same direction on price targets but landing in very different places on conviction. Barclays trimmed its target to $36 from $37 while maintaining an Overweight rating, while Truist cut its target to $30 from ... Carnival Stock Cut by Barclays and Truist, but This Wall Street Pro Still Sees Upside
